title: Pause
description: Pause: Built a mental health brand people actually loved by doing what no one else would — making them laugh.
subtitle: Built a mental health brand people actually loved by doing what no one else would — making them laugh.

sections.label: THE LANDSCAPE
sections.text: Mental health came with stigma, and the last thing people wanted was another earnest, overly-precious meditation app. Meanwhile the category was drowning in the same visual clichés — pastels, nature, soft whispers, and gentle hands resting on knees.

sections.label: THE INSIGHT
sections.text: Dark humour is how a lot of people actually deal with hard feelings. Instead of the expected "it's okay to not be okay" approach, we built Pause's brand voice around self-deprecating humour, honest emotional admission, and a tone that felt like talking to your sharpest friend — the one who makes you laugh about the thing you've been avoiding.

sections.label: THE OUTCOME
sections.text: When an entire category competes on sincerity, the funniest voice in the room wins. Pause launched with a 70+ NPS and a brand that didn't feel like a brand — it felt like a group chat. Users didn't just use the app, they shared it — because the content was worth sharing independent of the product.

title: Attis Insider
description: Attis Insider: Launched India's first biohacking community with a viral campaign and a bag of magic beans.
subtitle: Launched India's first biohacking community with a viral campaign and a bag of magic beans.

sections.label: THE LANDSCAPE
sections.text: India's biohacking space was nonexistent. There were no dedicated brands, no communities, no cultural conversation around the idea that optimisation wasn't just for athletes or tech bros. Attis wanted to build a community before selling a product — but "biohacking" sounded intimidating.

sections.label: THE INSIGHT
sections.text: We created Attis Insider — a membership-style community that made biohacking feel like a culture, not a science experiment. The launch campaign, "Magic Pills", was deliberately provocative: we sent mystery packages branded as pills to health influencers and content creators with no context.

sections.label: THE OUTCOME
sections.text: The campaign generated over 100k organic views, 500+ social mentions, and 1000+ community signups — all on ₹0 ad spend. The mystery packaging created FOMO-driven sharing loops that outperformed paid campaigns.

title: QAonCloud
description: QAonCloud: Repositioned, rebranded, and rebuilt a 12-year-old QA company from the ground up.
subtitle: Repositioned, rebranded, and rebuilt a 12-year-old QA company from the ground up.

sections.label: THE LANDSCAPE
sections.text: QAonCloud is a software testing company with 12+ years of experience and 60+ global clients. The problem wasn't the work — it was the brand. The existing website looked like every other generic IT services company: clip art icons, vague promises, and zero differentiation. In a market where every QA company says the same thing, QAonCloud had no reason for anyone to pick them over the next search result.

sections.label: THE INSIGHT
sections.text: We stripped everything back and found the one thing that was actually true and defensible: QAonCloud's AI-accelerated process genuinely delivered QA 40% faster than in-house teams. That became the entire brand. We repositioned from a generic "QA testing services company" to a specific, measurable promise — "40% faster QA. Accelerated by AI, driven by experts." Then we rebuilt the visual identity, rewrote every word on the site, and designed and developed a completely new web presence from scratch.

sections.label: THE OUTCOME
sections.text: The new site went from a generic enterprise template to a sharp, conversion-focused experience with clear positioning, real social proof, and a specific promise that no competitor was making. We also built a content strategy and outreach programme to drive inbound and outbound leads.

title: Origin One
description: Origin One: Increased sales by 153% in 4 weeks via user research, media buying & advertising.
subtitle: Increased sales by 153% in 4 weeks via user research, media buying & advertising.

sections.label: THE LANDSCAPE
sections.text: Origin One is a premium stationery brand with a product people loved once they tried it — but almost no one was trying it. Advertising existed but it was scattered and directionless. There was no clear positioning, no consistent creative, and no strategy behind what was being spent. The brand had strong social proof from existing customers but had never used it.

sections.label: THE INSIGHT
sections.text: Stationery isn't a rational purchase — nobody needs another notebook. It's an emotional one. We built a creative strategy around what the product actually made people feel: the texture of the paper, the ritual of writing, the quiet satisfaction of something beautifully made. We paired visceral, sensory-driven ads with real customer stories that had been sitting unused. Then we built the media buying infrastructure to put it all in front of the right people.

sections.label: THE OUTCOME
sections.text: Sales increased by 153% in 4 weeks. What started as an advertising engagement has since expanded into full CRO — optimising the funnel, improving the user experience, and turning one-time buyers into repeat customers.

title: QwikBuild
description: QwikBuild: GTM strategy & channel validation that generated the signal to pivot the product.
subtitle: GTM strategy & channel validation that generated the signal to pivot the product.

sections.label: THE LANDSCAPE
sections.text: QwikBuild is a consumer AI platform that lets anyone build production-ready apps without code. The product was built. The question was: how do you get it in front of people, and which people? There was no established GTM playbook and no clear sense of which channels, messages, or positioning would work.

sections.label: THE INSIGHT
sections.text: Instead of guessing, we tested everything. We ran advertising across Meta and Google, experimented with different positioning angles and messaging, tested product onboarding flows, explored PLG levers, and tried community-led adoption. Each experiment was designed to generate signal, not just traffic.

sections.label: THE OUTCOME
sections.text: Some channels worked. Some messaging landed. But the most valuable thing we uncovered wasn't a growth hack — it was product signal. The GTM experiments revealed enough about how people understood (and misunderstood) the product that it informed a pivot on the product itself. Sometimes the best thing a creative partner can do isn't make the numbers go up — it's help you figure out what to build next. A major relaunch is coming.

title: Attis Campaigns
description: Attis Campaigns: Two viral campaigns that exposed nerves. Zero ad spend. Massive reach.
subtitle: Two viral campaigns that exposed nerves. Zero ad spend. Massive reach.

sections.label: THE LANDSCAPE
sections.text: After building the community, Attis needed to convert attention into revenue. But typical health-brand advertising — clean product shots, benefit-focused copy, influencer unboxings — would have killed the edge that made the brand interesting.

sections.label: THE INSIGHT
sections.text: We ran two campaigns designed to expose nerves. Each one made people uncomfortable enough to stop scrolling, curious enough to click, and convinced enough to buy. The campaigns leaned into Attis's counter-cultural positioning rather than softening it for conversion.

sections.label: THE OUTCOME
sections.text: Two viral campaigns. 850+ product orders. 250k+ organic views. The brand maintained its edge while proving that provocative creative isn't just for awareness — it converts.
